description: A container that processes its childnode with a dynamic buffer size

parameterGroups.label: Block Size Control
parameterGroups.rationale: Single parameter controlling the processing block size. The value is an index into a fixed array of valid block sizes, not the block size itself.

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph. No effect on audio processing.

gaps.question: The existing doc lists the mapping as [1, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512] for indices 0-7. How are fractional parameter values handled? The doc says 'will round to next power of two' -- is this rounding done in setParameter or in the processing dispatch? What about negative values?
gaps.context: The parameter can be modulated. Users need to know exactly how modulation values map to block sizes, especially edge cases.

gaps.question: The infrastructure doc says dynamic_blocksize uses a SimpleReadWriteLock. When the block size parameter changes, does it acquire a write lock and call prepare() on children? What happens to in-progress audio processing -- does the try-read lock cause the current block to be skipped?
gaps.context: Critical for understanding whether block size changes are click-free or may cause brief audio dropouts.

gaps.question: When BlockSize index 0 (blockSize=1) is selected, the node switches to FrameConverters for per-sample processing. Does this trigger a re-prepare of children with blockSize=1? How does the transition from block mode to frame mode work at runtime?
gaps.context: The existing doc mentions this as 'bonus level'. Need to confirm that children see blockSize=1 in their PrepareSpecs and can adapt accordingly.

gaps.question: The existing doc says this compiles to 'a dynamic version that will branch between the different block sizes'. Does the compiled output use a switch statement over all 8 block size options, or is there an optimisation for the common case?
gaps.context: Users choosing between fix_blockx and dynamic_blocksize need to understand the performance trade-off after compilation.

gaps.question: The existing doc explains why block sizes 2 and 4 are excluded (below HISE_EVENT_RASTER=8). When BlockSize=1 (frame mode) is selected, does this also go below the event raster? How are MIDI event timestamps handled in that case?
gaps.context: BlockSize=1 is below HISE_EVENT_RASTER=8, yet it is explicitly supported. Need to understand if this is a special case or if the event raster warning only applies to intermediate sizes.

notes: The most feature-rich of the block-size containers. Key distinction from fix_blockx: BlockSize is a parameter (modulatable at runtime) rather than a property (design-time only). The HasFixedParameters cppProperty means the parameter list cannot be modified at runtime (no dynamic parameter addition/removal). The existing phase3 doc is excellent -- 52 lines with a comparison table, code example, and explanation of the index-to-blocksize mapping. The text converter in the base data encodes the named display values for the parameter (Frame, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512).
